source.notes: Imported from Azure DevOps work item 6399

observedSteps: User Story
As a customer, I want to open the Login page from the Home page and sign in with approved credentials so that I can access authenticated account features and logged-in functionality.
Application Context
WashTab is a single page application (SPA).
The workflow begins on the Home page at http://localhost/washtabui/home in guest state.
The Login page is not directly accessed by URL in this workflow and must be opened through the Home page person/profile button.
After successful authentication, the application returns the user to the Home page in authenticated state.
Entry Conditions
Launch the WashTab application.
Navigate to http://localhost/washtabui/home.
Verify the Home page is loaded in guest state.
Verify the user is unauthenticated before starting the workflow.
Workflow Scope
This workflow covers:
Opening the Login page from the Home page.
Validating Login page controls and navigation behavior.
Performing authentication using approved credentials.
Validating invalid login behavior.
Validating password masking behavior.
Validating whitespace handling behavior.
Verifying successful return to the Home page in authenticated state.
Verifying failed authentication keeps the user unauthenticated.
Verifying that all authentication failures display only the generic Failed message.
Primary Navigation Journey
Start on the Home page in guest state.
Click the person/profile button to open the Login page.
Validate Login page elements and behavior.
Enter Username and Password credentials.
Submit the login request using the Login button.
On successful authentication, return to the Home page in authenticated state with authenticated-only UI visible.
On failed authentication, including blank credentials, partial credentials, or invalid credentials, remain unauthenticated and display the generic Failed message.
Use the Back button or Home button on the Login page to return to the Home page without authentication.
Approved Test Data
Approved positive login credentials:
Username: krisadmin
Password: Carwash#1
These credentials are approved for positive authentication scenarios and should be reused consistently across manual and automation assets.
Page / Component Elements
Home Page:
Person/profile button
Authenticated user identity display
Authenticated-only feature area
Login Page:
Username field
Password field
Login button
Back button
Home button
Password masking behavior
Generic Failed authentication message
Business Rules
The Login page must be opened through the Home page person/profile button in this workflow.
Password input must be masked by default.
Valid credentials must authenticate the user successfully.
Invalid credentials must not authenticate the user.
Any authentication failure, including blank username, blank password, partial credentials, or incorrect credentials, must display only the generic Failed message.
Separate required-field validation messaging must not be displayed.
Failed authentication must keep the user unauthenticated.
Leading and trailing whitespace handling must behave according to implemented business behavior and be validated.
Back and Home buttons on the Login page must return the user to the Home page.
Successful authentication must return the user to the Home page in authenticated state.
Validation Expectations
The Login page opens successfully from the Home page.
Username, Password, and Login controls are visible and interactive.
Back and Home buttons are visible and functional.
Password values are masked during entry.
Invalid credentials display the generic Failed message.
Blank username and/or blank password submissions display the generic Failed message.
No separate required-field validation message is displayed.
Failed login does not authenticate the user.
Successful login using approved credentials returns the user to authenticated Home state.
Authenticated user identity and authenticated-only features are visible after successful login.
Whitespace entry behavior is validated according to implemented business behavior.
Transition Expectations
Clicking the person/profile button transitions the application from Home page guest state to the Login page.
Successful login transitions the application from Login page to authenticated Home page state.
Failed login keeps the user on the Login page or otherwise unauthenticated.
Authentication failures, including blank field submissions, prevent authentication and maintain unauthenticated state.
Back button and Home button navigation return the user to the Home page without authentication.
Resource Reuse Guidance
Reuse the existing Home page resource for:
Home page launch and validation
Person/profile button interaction
Authenticated Home state validation
Reuse the existing Login page resource for:
Credential entry
Login submission
Login validation behavior
Generic Failed message validation
Reuse shared or upstream navigation ownership for Back and Home button behavior when already available instead of duplicating ownership.
Downstream Automation Guidance
Preserve the Home-to-Login-to-Home navigation sequence across generated manual and automation assets.
Use the approved credentials consistently for all positive authentication scenarios.
Keep authentication state validation explicit before and after login actions.
Validate that all authentication failures return the same generic Failed message.
Do not generate separate required-field validation checks because the application does not display dedicated required-field messages.
Prefer reuse of existing shared page resources, reusable actions, and upstream ownership boundaries over introducing duplicate workflow ownership.
Ensure downstream assets validate both authenticated and unauthenticated application states.
Ensure negative validation coverage includes invalid credentials, blank submissions, partial credential submissions, and whitespace handling behavior.
Acceptance Criteria
Given the user is on the Home page in guest state, when the user clicks the person/profile button, then the Login page should open.
Given the user is on the Login page, when the user enters Username and Password values, then the Password field should display masked input behavior.
Given the user is on the Login page, when the user enters approved valid credentials and clicks Login, then the user should be authenticated and returned to the Home page in authenticated state.

Given the user is on the Login page, when invalid credentials are submitted, then authentication should fail, the generic Failed message should be displayed, and the user should remain unauthenticated.

Given the user is on the Login page, when Login is submitted with both Username and Password empty, then authentication should fail, the generic Failed message should be displayed, and the user should remain unauthenticated.

Given the user is on the Login page, when Login is submitted with only Username or only Password populated, then authentication should fail, the generic Failed message should be displayed, and the user should remain unauthenticated.

Given the user is on the Login page, when credential values containing leading or trailing spaces are submitted, then the application should handle the values according to implemented business behavior and the observed result should be validated.
Given the user is on the Login page, when the Back button is clicked, then the user should return to the Home page in unauthenticated state.
Given the user is on the Login page, when the Home button is clicked, then the user should return to the Home page in unauthenticated state.

externalContext.title: Login Feature
externalContext.description:
1. User Story
 
 As a customer, I want to open the Login page from the Home page and sign in with approved credentials so that I can access authenticated account features and logged-in functionality.
 
 2. Application Context
 
 WashTab is a single page application (SPA).
 
 The workflow begins on the Home page at http://localhost/washtabui/home in guest state.
 
 The Login page is not directly accessed by URL in this workflow and must be opened through the Home page person/profile button.
 
 After successful authentication, the application returns the user to the Home page in authenticated state.
 
 3. Entry Conditions
 
 - Launch the WashTab application.
 - Navigate to http://localhost/washtabui/home.
 - Verify the Home page is loaded in guest state.
 - Verify the user is unauthenticated before starting the workflow.
 
 4. Workflow Scope
 
 This workflow covers:
 - Opening the Login page from the Home page.
 - Validating Login page controls and navigation behavior.
 - Performing authentication using approved credentials.
 - Validating invalid login behavior.
 - Validating password masking behavior.
 - Validating whitespace handling behavior.
 - Verifying successful return to the Home page in authenticated state.
 - Verifying failed authentication keeps the user unauthenticated.
 - Verifying that all authentication failures display only the generic Failed message.
 
 5. Primary Navigation Journey
 
 - Start on the Home page in guest state.
 - Click the person/profile button to open the Login page.
 - Validate Login page elements and behavior.
 - Enter Username and Password credentials.
 - Submit the login request using the Login button.
 - On successful authentication, return to the Home page in authenticated state with authenticated-only UI visible.
 - On failed authentication, including blank credentials, partial credentials, or invalid credentials, remain unauthenticated and display the generic Failed message.
 - Use the Back button or Home button on the Login page to return to the Home page without authentication.
 
 6. Approved Test Data
 
 Approved positive login credentials:
 - Username: krisadmin
 - Password: Carwash#1
 
 These credentials are approved for positive authentication scenarios and should be reused consistently across manual and automation assets.
 
 7. Page / Component Elements
 
 Home Page:
 - Person/profile button
 - Authenticated user identity display
 - Authenticated-only feature area
 
 Login Page:
 - Username field
 - Password field
 - Login button
 - Back button
 - Home button
 - Password masking behavior
 - Generic Failed authentication message
 
 8. Business Rules
 
 - The Login page must be opened through the Home page person/profile button in this workflow.
 - Password input must be masked by default.
 - Valid credentials must authenticate the user successfully.
 - Invalid credentials must not authenticate the user.
 - Any authentication failure, including blank username, blank password, partial credentials, or incorrect credentials, must display only the generic Failed message.
 - Separate required-field validation messaging must not be displayed.
 - Failed authentication must keep the user unauthenticated.
 - Leading and trailing whitespace handling must behave according to implemented business behavior and be validated.
 - Back and Home buttons on the Login page must return the user to the Home page.
 - Successful authentication must return the user to the Home page in authenticated state.
 
 9. Validation Expectations
 
 - The Login page opens successfully from the Home page.
 - Username, Password, and Login controls are visible and interactive.
 - Back and Home buttons are visible and functional.
 - Password values are masked during entry.
 - Invalid credentials display the generic Failed message.
 - Blank username and/or blank password submissions display the generic Failed message.
 - No separate required-field validation message is displayed.
 - Failed login does not authenticate the user.
 - Successful login using approved credentials returns the user to authenticated Home state.
 - Authenticated user identity and authenticated-only features are visible after successful login.
 - Whitespace entry behavior is validated according to implemented business behavior.
 
 10. Transition Expectations
 
 - Clicking the person/profile button transitions the application from Home page guest state to the Login page.
 - Successful login transitions the application from Login page to authenticated Home page state.
 - Failed login keeps the user on the Login page or otherwise unauthenticated.
 - Authentication failures, including blank field submissions, prevent authentication and maintain unauthenticated state.
 - Back button and Home button navigation return the user to the Home page without authentication.
 
 11. Resource Reuse Guidance
 
 - Reuse the existing Home page resource for:
 - Home page launch and validation
 - Person/profile button interaction
 - Authenticated Home state validation
 - Reuse the existing Login page resource for:
 - Credential entry
 - Login submission
 - Login validation behavior
 - Generic Failed message validation
 - Reuse shared or upstream navigation ownership for Back and Home button behavior when already available instead of duplicating ownership.
 
 12. Downstream Automation Guidance
 
 - Preserve the Home-to-Login-to-Home navigation sequence across generated manual and automation assets.
 - Use the approved credentials consistently for all positive authentication scenarios.
 - Keep authentication state validation explicit before and after login actions.
 - Validate that all authentication failures return the same generic Failed message.
 - Do not generate separate required-field validation checks because the application does not display dedicated required-field messages.
 - Prefer reuse of existing shared page resources, reusable actions, and upstream ownership boundaries over introducing duplicate workflow ownership.
 - Ensure downstream assets validate both authenticated and unauthenticated application states.
 - Ensure negative validation coverage includes invalid credentials, blank submissions, partial credential submissions, and whitespace handling behavior.
 
 13. Acceptance Criteria
 
 - Given the user is on the Home page in guest state, when the user clicks the person/profile button, then the Login page should open.
 - Given the user is on the Login page, when the user enters Username and Password values, then the Password field should display masked input behavior.
 - Given the user is on the Login page, when the user enters approved valid credentials and clicks Login, then the user should be authenticated and returned to the Home page in authenticated state. - Given the user is on the Login page, when invalid credentials are submitted, then authentication should fail, the generic Failed message should be displayed, and the user should remain unauthenticated.
 - Given the user is on the Login page, when Login is submitted with both Username and Password empty, then authentication should fail, the generic Failed message should be displayed, and the user should remain unauthenticated.
 - Given the user is on the Login page, when Login is submitted with only Username or only Password populated, then authentication should fail, the generic Failed message should be displayed, and the user should remain unauthenticated.
 - Given the user is on the Login page, when credential values containing leading or trailing spaces are submitted, then the application should handle the values according to implemented business behavior and the observed result should be validated.
 - Given the user is on the Login page, when the Back button is clicked, then the user should return to the Home page in unauthenticated state.
 - Given the user is on the Login page, when the Home button is clicked, then the user should return to the Home page in unauthenticated state.
